notes: Confirmed: SKGLView.Dispose() is declared as 'public void Dispose()' (non-virtual) on line 190 of SKGLView.razor.cs. The class is not sealed, so subclassing is valid, but subclasses cannot override Dispose to add custom cleanup logic. SKCanvasView has the identical pattern (line 171 of SKCanvasView.razor.cs). Neither class implements the standard .NET Dispose(bool disposing) pattern. The reporter's enhancement request is valid.

reproductionSteps.description: Check SKGLView class declaration and Dispose method for virtual keyword
reproductionSteps.output:

reproductionSteps.output:

171:		public void Dispose()

reproductionSteps.description: Verify no Dispose(bool) pattern exists in either Blazor view — grep returns no matches confirming absence
reproductionSteps.output: (no matches — Dispose(bool) pattern is absent from both views)

actionability.reason: Enhancement confirmed: both Blazor views use non-virtual Dispose without the standard Dispose(bool) pattern. Implementation is a small, well-scoped change to adopt the standard .NET IDisposable pattern.

workarounds: Use 'public new void Dispose()' in subclass to hide base Dispose and call base.Dispose() manually (does not work polymorphically)
